purpose: Handles hackathon and program prize pools with batch payouts, dependency management, and circuit breaker protection.

public.name: init_program
public.description: Initialize a new program escrow instance.

public.name: lock_program_funds
public.description: Lock funds into the program escrow. Blocked when lock_paused is true.

public.name: single_payout
public.description: Execute a single payout to one winner. Blocked when circuit breaker is OPEN or release_paused is true.

public.name: batch_payout
public.description: Execute batch payouts to multiple winners atomically (all-or-nothing). Blocked when circuit breaker is OPEN or release_paused is true.

public.name: get_circuit_breaker_status
public.description: Return a full snapshot of the circuit breaker state including is_open, failure_count, success_count, and configured thresholds.

public.name: get_cb_schema_version
public.description: Return the upgrade-safe circuit-breaker storage schema version. Returns 0 on legacy deployments.

public.name: get_circuit_error_log
public.description: Return the full circuit breaker error log (last N entries).

admin.name: initialize_contract
admin.description: Initialize the contract with an admin address.

admin.name: set_circuit_admin
admin.description: Set or rotate the circuit breaker admin address.
admin.authorization: existing circuit admin or contract admin

admin.name: emergency_open_circuit
admin.description: Immediately transition the circuit breaker to OPEN, blocking all payouts. Use when a security incident is detected.

admin.name: reset_circuit_breaker
admin.description: Reset the circuit breaker to CLOSED state, allowing payouts to resume.

admin.name: configure_circuit_breaker
admin.description: Configure circuit breaker thresholds: failure_threshold, success_threshold, and max_error_log entries.

admin.name: set_paused
admin.description: Set pause flags for lock, release, and refund operations independently.

admin.name: set_program_spend_threshold
admin.description: Set per-program spend threshold. Any single payout or batch total exceeding this is rejected.

circuit_breaker.description: Three-state circuit breaker (Closed, Open, HalfOpen) guards all payout operations with deterministic, explicit errors and upgrade-safe storage.

Closed.description: Normal operation. Requests pass through after basic checks.

Open.description: Circuit open (protection active). All payout requests rejected immediately with ERR_CIRCUIT_OPEN without balance/threshold checks.

transitions.to_HalfOpen: admin calls reset_circuit_breaker() — moves Open -> HalfOpen for recovery attempt

HalfOpen.description: Admin has initiated recovery. Operations allowed but monitored. First failure re-opens; success_threshold consecutive successes close circuit.

deterministic_enforcement_order: 1. Reentrancy guard (acquire before any state read)
2. Idempotency key check (early exit if already processed)
6. CIRCUIT BREAKER CHECK (check_and_allow_with_thresholds before all business logic)
8. Input validation (amounts, recipient count, empty batch)

ERR_THRESHOLD_BREACHED.when: Threshold monitoring detects breach; circuit auto-opens

upgrade_safety.behavior_on_legacy: Returns 0 on deployments where marker was never written
upgrade_safety.migration_path: Version 0 -> 1 supported transparently; future versions trigger controlled failure

behaviors.atomicity: batch_payout uses all-or-nothing semantics. If any validation fails, no transfers occur and balance is unchanged.
behaviors.idempotency: Optional idempotency key on single_payout and batch_payout ensures deterministic behavior on retries.
behaviors.reentrancy_protection: All payout paths acquire a reentrancy guard before any state read.

storage_keys.CircuitBreakerSchemaVersion: Upgrade-safe schema version marker for circuit breaker storage. Written on init.
storage_keys.PauseFlags: PauseFlags struct with lock_paused, release_paused, refund_paused.

security_notes: Circuit breaker check runs before balance and threshold checks for deterministic, stable rejection messages.
emergency_open_circuit halts all payouts immediately without waiting for failure threshold.
Only the circuit admin can open or reset the circuit breaker.
Token economics (fee-on-transfer tokens) are out of scope per external_calls.rs.
All payout rejections emit audit events before panicking so the rejection is always visible on-chain.

circuit_breaker_test_cases: CB-04: Open circuit — no partial transfer
CB-07: Status reflects CLOSED after reset
CB-08: Only circuit admin can emergency open
CB-09: Only circuit admin can reset
CB-10: Schema version readable after init
CB-11: Open circuit — batch no partial transfer
CB-14: Multiple payouts succeed after reset
CB-18: Emergency open emits audit event
